mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in industrial production indexes reflects accelerating manufacturing and utility output, which raises corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or positive return hit rate in top quintile is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate indicates weakening labor demand that precedes consumption slowdowns and earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not statistically negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated federal funds rate and falling retail sales captures a restrictive monetary policy actively constraining consumer demand, a combination that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS level and RSAFS change fails to achieve a t-statistic magnitude greater than 2.0 in predicting 20-day returns, or the signal produces positive returns in > 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening regimes, month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation reinforces expectations of additional rate hikes, raising discount rates and dragging S&P 500 returns lower over the next week.
failure_condition: In identified tightening regimes, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes are associated with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or the conditional mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding threshold levels signal robust residential construction activity and durable goods demand, supporting cyclical corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average conditional return is not positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side stress, which reduces aggregate earnings forecasts and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month payroll growth alongside a restrictive federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates a resilient labor market forcing prolonged monetary restriction, raising hard-landing odds and producing negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction of PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS level fails to produce a statistically negative mean 20-day return (p > 0.05), or the signal generates positive returns in > 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of positive real GDP growth and moderating consumer price inflation defines a goldilocks macro environment where corporate revenues expand without margin pressure from cost shocks, lifting S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth positive and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.3%
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term does not exhibit a t-statistic greater than 2.0 in a forward-return regression.
